title: Order Events
description: The Order events reference details the various triggers and notifications associated with order processes. These events provide real-time updates on the status and progress of orders, from initiation to completion. By monitoring order events, you can obtain insights into the payment flow, detect issues, and optimize the checkout experience. Each event is linked to specific order attributes, enabling detailed tracking, ensuring seamless order and payment management.

apis.heading: Order Updated
apis.description: This event is triggered when an order is updated.

reqParams.name: eventType
reqParams.description: Denotes the category of the webhook event.

reqParams.name: metadata
reqParams.description: Includes information about the webhook event like the unique event ID, the date and time the event was created (timestamp), the number of retry attempts made to send the event, and the specific webhook event ID denoting the type of event.

reqParams.name: eventId
reqParams.description: Unique ID of the webhook event.

reqParams.name: created
reqParams.description: Timestamp at which the event was created.

reqParams.name: retryAttempt
reqParams.description: Number of retry attempts made for the webhook event. Surfboard will attempt to post messages three times in case of failures, with a delay of 5 minutes between the first and second attempts, and a delay of 10 minutes between the second and third attempts.

reqParams.name: terminalId
reqParams.description: Identifier of the terminal that triggered this event.

reqParams.name: originalCreated
reqParams.description: Timestamp of the very first time the event was published

reqParams.name: webhookEventId
reqParams.description: Unique ID of the webhook event.

reqParams.name: data
reqParams.description: The payload in the webhook event that contains the core information or updates being delivered. This is the useful data that recipients can act on.

reqParams.name: orderId
reqParams.description: Unique identifier for the order created.

reqParams.name: merchantId
reqParams.description: Unique identifier of the merchant.

reqParams.name: adjustments
reqParams.description: List of adjustments applied to the order.

resParams.name: adjustmentType
resParams.description: Category or reason for the adjustment.

resParams.name: amount
resParams.description: Amount of the adjustment in minor currency units.

reqParams.name: referenceId
reqParams.description: External referenceId passed during order creation.

reqParams.name: metadata
reqParams.description: Metadata passed with the order creation.

reqParams.name: paymentStatus
reqParams.description: Provides the status of the payment. For this event, the status is 'PAYMENT_INITIATED'.

reqParams.name: transactionDetails
reqParams.description: Retrieves all transaction details for the order.

reqParams.name: transactionId
reqParams.description: Unique identifier for the transaction.

reqParams.name: terminalId
reqParams.description: Identifier of the payment terminal from which the transaction was made.

reqParams.name: rrn
reqParams.description: Retrieval Reference Number (RRN) used to uniquely identify a card transaction.

reqParams.name: amount
reqParams.description: Payment amount in minor currency units (e.g., 110 for 1.10 in SEK).

reqParams.name: currency
reqParams.description: Numeric or ISO code for the currency (e.g., 752 for SEK).

reqParams.name: method
reqParams.description: Payment method used for the transaction (e.g., CARD).

reqParams.name: truncatedPan
reqParams.description: Last few digits of the card number.

reqParams.name: cardLabel
reqParams.description: Descriptive label of the card or application (e.g., 'Debit Mastercard').

reqParams.name: posEntryMode
reqParams.description: Specifies how the card data was read (e.g., contact, contactless).

reqParams.name: aid
reqParams.description: Application Identifier (AID) for the card application.

reqParams.name: customerResponseCode
reqParams.description: Two-character response code representing authorization result (e.g., '00' for approved).

reqParams.name: cvmMethod
reqParams.description: Indicates the cardholder verification method used (e.g., '1f').

reqParams.name: authMode
reqParams.description: Indicates the entity that authenticated the payment (e.g., ISSUER).

reqParams.name: cardBrand
reqParams.description: Normalized brand of the card (e.g., MASTERCARD, VISA).

reqParams.name: terminalVerificationResult
reqParams.description: Verification outcomes from the terminal (e.g., '0000000001').

reqParams.name: cvmMethodDescription
reqParams.description: Descriptive explanation of the CVM method (e.g., 'Offline plaintext PIN').

reqParams.name: posEntryMode

reqParams.name: cardBrand
reqParams.description: Normalized brand of the card used (e.g., 'VISA', 'MASTERCARD').

reqParams.name: terminalVerificationResult
reqParams.description: Result code from the terminal’s EMV verification process.

reqParams.name: customerResponseDescription
reqParams.description: A message from the payment processor explaining the transaction outcome to the customer.

apis.heading: Order Customer Identity
apis.description: This event is triggered when a customer taps their card on the terminal, enabling you to identify the customer during a transaction and personalize the experience.

reqParams.name: eventType
reqParams.description: Denotes the category of the webhook event.

reqParams.name: metadata
reqParams.description: Contains metadata about the webhook delivery itself, including unique identifiers, timestamps, and retry counts.

reqParams.name: eventId
reqParams.description: Unique ID for this specific webhook event.

reqParams.name: created
reqParams.description: Timestamp at which the event was created.

reqParams.name: retryAttempt
reqParams.description: Number of retry attempts made for the webhook event. Surfboard will attempt to post messages three times in case of failures, with a delay of 5 minutes between the first and second attempts, and a delay of 10 minutes between the second and third attempts

reqParams.name: webhookEventId
reqParams.description: Unique ID of the webhook event.

reqParams.name: data
reqParams.description: Holds the actual event payload — details about the order and the card token used to identify the customer.

reqParams.name: orderId
reqParams.description: Unique identifier for the order associated with the identified customer.

reqParams.name: cardId
reqParams.description: Tokenized identifier for the customer's card used to recognize or link the customer to the order.

apis.heading: Order Terminal Event
apis.description: This event is triggered for every state the terminal undergoes during a transaction.

reqParams.description: Denotes the category of the webhook event.

reqParams.name: webhookEventId
reqParams.description: Unique ID of the webhook event.

reqParams.name: data
reqParams.description: The payload in the webhook event that contains the core information or updates being delivered. This is the useful data that recipients can act on.

reqParams.name: orderId
reqParams.description: Unique identifier for the order.

reqParams.name: merchantId
reqParams.description: Unique identifier of the merchant.

reqParams.name: paymentId
reqParams.description: Unique identifier for the payment.

reqParams.name: terminalTransactionStatus
reqParams.description: Indicates the terminal's current status in relation to the transaction. Some of these states are only available for an online terminal as mentioned below.

possibleValues.description: Transaction is initiated on the terminal.
Tip selection screen is displayed for customer to select tip.
Waiting for card tap/insert.
Customer has presented card.
Application needs to select an application as card has more than one application present.
Customer needs to enter PIN
Wrong pin entered by customer
Payment authorization initiated from terminal
Authorization submitted to the backend
Authorization process is complete
[ONLINE ONLY] Payment page has fully loaded.
[ONLINE ONLY] The page is now ready for card details
[ONLINE ONLY] Google Pay SDK is available and has been successfully mounted.
[ONLINE ONLY] Apple Pay SDK is available and has been successfully mounted
[ONLINE ONLY] The customer has started entering information in the online page.
[ONLINE ONLY] Card payment is initiated
[ONLINE ONLY] Customer has initiated an Apple Pay attempt.
[ONLINE ONLY] The customer has initiated a Google Pay attempt
[ONLINE ONLY] The Apple Pay payment process is initiated.
[ONLINE ONLY] The Google Pay payment is initiated.

reqParams.name: orderStatus
reqParams.description: Provides the status of the order.

reqParams.name: metadata
reqParams.description: Metadata passed with the order creation.
